title: Matrix Build Priority Scheduling
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: matrix build priority scheduling.
assumptions: Resources (runners, storage slots, scanners) are allocated exclusively and must be released before reuse.
Compatibility mappings (e.g., runner↔job, test_profile↔job) are predefined and correct in the inventory.
Approvals and automated policies are deterministic for a given job state; there are no probabilistic approvals.
Quality and security checks produce boolean pass/fail results that drive gate progression.
risks: Resource starvation: high-priority jobs may exhaust runners or storage, blocking other pipelines.
Approval bottlenecks: manual approver scarcity can delay promotions and reduce throughput.
False negatives/positives in scanners or analysis tools could incorrectly promote or block jobs.
Complex cross-resource dependencies increase the risk of deadlock if reservations are not released atomically.
